name: sprint-1b-discord-resident
idea:
# Sprint 1b — Discord resident mode + robustness

Add the second adapter for each port — Discord transport, Supabase store. This proves the port abstractions hold (two impls each) and unlocks resident-mode features: coalescing, recovery, voice/image attachments, live status messages.

**Full spec is at `planning-bot-spec.md` in this repo root. Refer to it for complete data model schemas, tool signatures, and architectural details. Especially the sections: Execution Modes, Multi-Message Handling, Status Message, Idempotency and Recovery, Images.**

## Supabase
- URL: https://yhwflvadmefhkshwbfnf.supabase.co
- Service key: eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJzdXBhYmFzZSIsInJlZiI6Inlod2ZsdmFkbWVmaGtzaHdiZm5mIiwicm9sZSI6InNlcnZpY2Vfcm9sZSIsImlhdCI6MTc3NzUwOTQ2NywiZXhwIjoyMDkzMDg1NDY3fQ.xFknK1AD9JF6JIcGTYYOyOLUQNNj_WMNGL08K9Xr2NY

## Scope

- Railway + Supabase setup; Supabase CLI for local dev
- First Supabase migration files mirroring SQLite schema from Sprint 1a; `supabase db push` workflow
- Supabase store adapter (second Store impl); same contract tests from 1a run green against both stores
- Discord transport adapter (push, second Transport impl): bot account via discord.py; on_message handler for DMs; user whitelist
- Multi-message coalescing — 10s window, burst handling (resident-only)
- Restart safety: messages persisted on receipt; recovery routine at startup + every 5min; abandoned turns marked; `external_requests` ledger table; idempotency-key generation (sha256-based); per-provider reconciliation
- Voice message support: Groq Whisper (whisper-large-v3) integration; transcription stored in messages.content; original audio in Supabase Storage
- Image attachment handling: Discord image detection; download to Supabase Storage; create images row with source='user_uploaded'; auto-assigned reference_key
- Image tools: list_images, view_image, send_image, update_image_metadata
- Live status message: loop sends status at turn start, edits after every tool call with count + last 3 tools + dynamic timestamp `<t:UNIX:R>`; set_activity tool annotates current step
- Mid-turn message handling: messages persist immediately; status message gets annotation; bot prompted with mid-turn messages

## Key New Tables

### external_requests
id, idempotency_key (unique), provider, endpoint, tool_call_id, turn_id, request_summary (json), status (pending|sent|confirmed|failed|orphaned), provider_request_id, provider_response_summary (json), attempt_count, first_attempted_at, last_attempted_at, completed_at, error_details (json)

Idempotency key: sha256(turn_id:tool_call_id:provider:endpoint:canonical_args)[:16]

### images
id, epic_id, source (agent_generated|user_uploaded), prompt, storage_url, quality, size, created_at, reference_key (unique per epic), description, caption, in_body, active (default true), discord_attachment_id

## Acceptance Criteria

- DM bot from whitelisted account → response within 30s
- DM from non-whitelisted → no response, log entry
- Every inbound message persists with unique discord_message_id
- Store port contract test suite runs green against Supabase impl unchanged from SQLite
- 5 messages in 8s → processed as single burst (triggered_by_message_ids has 5 entries)
- Kill server mid-turn, restart → triggering messages requeued under fresh turn; previous turn marked abandoned
- Voice message (mocked) → transcribed via mocked Groq, was_voice_message=true
- Image attachment (mocked) → downloaded to Storage, images row created with source=user_uploaded
- view_image returns image bytes via Anthropic vision
- send_image posts to Discord with caption
- Status message: turn starts → status sent; each tool call → edited; set_activity updates; turn completes → "Done. N tool calls."
- Mid-turn message: persists immediately; status gets annotation; bot prompted with mid-turn messages

## Tech Stack Additions
- discord.py for Discord gateway
- supabase-py for Supabase client
- groq SDK for Whisper transcription
- Supabase Storage for blob storage

flags_addressed.reason: Replaced the follow-up-turn shortcut with the spec's same-turn end-of-turn re-prompt via the new `on_pre_finalize` hook in `run_turn` (Step 6); resident runner queries unprocessed messages and `run_turn` widens `triggered_by_message_ids` retroactively before any final send.
Step 7 makes `send_message` mode-divergent via a new optional `transport` field on `ToolContext`. Resident path queues a Discord post through the external-queue mechanism added in Step 8.

Step 6 adds `on_turn_start: Callable[[turn_dict], None]` to `run_turn`, invoked synchronously after `create_turn`. Resident runner uses it to post the initial status message and persist `bot_turns.status_message_id` (Step 14.3).

Step 2 prohibits committing secrets, adds .gitignore entries, requires env-only access in adapters, and records a user action item to rotate the leaked Supabase service key. No literal keys appear in any plan-driven file.

Step 5 extends `Model.complete_turn` with `idempotency_key` and updates `AnthropicModel` to forward it as the `Idempotency-Key` header. The loop already has the key from `Ledger.record_pending`; just threads it through. `Reconciler` (Step 16) replays using the stored ledger key.

Narrowed scope: image and voice work this sprint is Discord-only. Invocation-mode attachments (`--attach`, `run_turn(attachments=)`, `LocalBlobStore`, caller-uploaded images per spec 1895-1908) remain explicitly deferred with a note in Step 22.2. The plan does not broaden Blob/image surface beyond Discord ingestion + the four image tools.

Reverted the change to `tests/store_contract.py` — it stays unchanged from Sprint 1a (Step 20.1). New Sprint 1b method coverage moved to a separate `tests/store_contract_v1b.py` (Step 20.2). Both stores now demonstrably pass the original contract suite without modification.

Same as FLAG-001: spec's same-turn end-of-turn check is implemented via the `on_pre_finalize` hook; the prior turn's `triggered_by_message_ids` is widened retroactively, satisfying spec lines 822-843.
Same as FLAG-008: scope contained. Invocation-mode attachment debt explicitly deferred and recorded in Step 22.2 rather than expanded.
Step 1 adds `psycopg[binary]>=3.1` to runtime dependencies.
Same as FLAG-007: `Model.complete_turn` gains `idempotency_key`; Anthropic adapter forwards it; reconciler replays via the model's normal API rather than bypassing it.

Step 8 restructures `audit_wrap` into three stages: tool body (no txn), DB-only audit txn (commits before any network IO), and post-commit external queue. Network IO never holds a transaction. Spec ordering at lines 2473-2480 is followed.
Step 6's `on_turn_start` hook gives the resident runner the `turn_id` it needs to post the initial status message before tool calls. Step 14.3 wires this end-to-end.

Step 6's `on_pre_finalize` hook prevents `run_turn` from finalizing while unprocessed mid-turn messages exist; the model is re-prompted with the spec block, in the same turn, before any `send_message` fires.
Plan now explicitly states (Overview + Step 22.2) that voice support this sprint is Discord ingestion only; the `transcribe_voice` tool and the non-voice-audio adversarial path are deferred.

Step 7 extends `ToolContext` with optional `transport`, `blob`, and `external_queue` fields. Image tools and resident `send_message` consume these; invocation-mode tools see `None` and behave unchanged.
Step 9 adds a NEW `PushTransport` Protocol rather than mutating the existing pull-style `Transport`. CLI continues to bypass `Transport` as it does today; no broad call-site updates required.

Step 6 adds `triggered_by_message_ids` AND `recovered_input_messages` to `run_turn`; Step 9 adds `Store.load_messages(message_ids)`. Resident dispatch and recovery rebuild input from persisted message rows rather than letting `run_turn` synthesize a fresh inbound message.

Same as FLAG-002: `send_message` is mode-divergent via injected transport. The auto-invocation path at `agent_kit/loop.py:264` continues to call `send_message`, which now routes to Discord in resident mode and to the reply buffer in invocation mode.

Step 5.3 persists `system_seq` into `external_requests.request_summary`. Step 16 replays via the row's stored `idempotency_key` directly (rather than re-deriving), which is the more robust path; the persisted `system_seq` is retained for diagnostic and audit purposes.

Step 4 adds a `request_body` JSONB column to `external_requests`; Step 5 has the loop persist the full canonical Anthropic body (`model`, `messages`, `tools`, `max_tokens`) at `record_pending` time; Step 16 reconstructs the Anthropic replay from this column rather than from the small `request_summary`. Anthropic dedupe via the stored `idempotency_key` works against the durable body.

Step 8 keeps the tool body INSIDE the existing `store.transaction()` exactly as in Sprint 1a. Only post-commit network callables are queued. The existing rollback test at `tests/test_tool_kit.py:27` is preserved verbatim and listed as a test that must keep passing.

Same as FLAG-007: durable `request_body` column gives the reconciler the full message/tool body needed to reissue with the stored idempotency key. Plan also adds a dedicated `tests/test_anthropic_replay.py` that asserts the stub Anthropic receives the original body.
Audit wrapper redesigned to keep mutation+tool_calls atomic in the same transaction as before. Network IO is the only thing moved post-commit. Existing rollback semantics are preserved.

Step 6.2 makes `run_turn` run the mid-turn check at TWO checkpoints: (a) before final-text auto-send, and (b) BEFORE each explicit `send_message` tool invocation. An explicit model-issued `send_message` cannot post to Discord while unprocessed mid-turn messages exist.

Step 13.2 makes `DiscordTransport` take a `Ledger` and inserts pending `external_requests` rows for every ingestion-side IO call (Storage upload, Groq transcription) keyed off `discord_message_id` (new `ingest_message_id` branch in `derive_idempotency_key`). Step 13.4 walks through the per-attachment ledger sequence. Recovery scan (Step 16) reconciles them like any other pending row.

Same as FLAG-012: ingestion-side IO is now ledgered. Restart safety covers voice transcription, Storage upload, and image upload; the reconciler's per-provider dispatch already handles `groq` and `supabase_storage` rows.
Same as FLAG-011: the mid-turn check now gates explicit `send_message` tool calls in addition to the final-text path. The 'still has uncovered path' from iteration 2 is closed.

Step 9.2 adds `Blob.exists(ref) -> bool` to the `Blob` Protocol. Step 9.3 adds `fetch_recent_messages(channel_id, since, until) -> list[JSONDict]` to the new `PushTransport` Protocol. Both methods are now part of the published interfaces the reconciler calls.

Same as correctness-1 / FLAG-009: tool body still runs inside the audit transaction, so the `ToolRegistry.invoke` caller contract is unchanged. Existing tools that mutate the store directly (e.g. `send_message` invocation path) keep working without refactoring. The new external-queue mechanism is purely additive: tools that don't queue anything see no behavioral change.

Step 13.4 reorders ingestion: every voice and image branch now does `store.create_message(...)` FIRST in transaction 1 (with the unique `discord_message_id`), THEN runs Storage/Groq/image-row external IO, THEN `store.update_message(...)` to fill post-processing fields. Spec lines 79 and 2455-2459 are honored: gateway-replay duplicates collide on the unique constraint immediately; recovery sees persisted-but-unprocessed messages.

Step 9.1 adds `Store.update_message(message_id, **changes)` to the Protocol. Step 10.3 and Step 11.3 implement it on both stores. Three consumers now use it: resident `send_message` (Step 7.2 fills `discord_message_id` post-confirm), voice ingestion (Step 13.4 fills `content`/`audio_storage_url`/`transcription_metadata` post-Groq), `send_image` (Step 17 fills `discord_message_id` post-Discord-post).

Same as FLAG-013: ingestion order is now persist-first. Plan also wraps the inbound `messages` insert + ingestion ledger pending rows in a single transaction so a crash before external IO leaves a recoverable state.

Same as FLAG-013: image ingestion path is also persist-first (Step 13.4 image branch). The `messages` row with `has_image_attachment=True` exists before any Storage upload runs, so duplicate gateway events collide on the unique constraint and no orphaned Storage/image-row work happens.

Same as FLAG-014: `update_message` is now part of the Store Protocol, implemented on both stores, and tested (`tests/test_update_message.py`). All three consumer flows (resident `send_message`, voice ingestion, `send_image`) call it explicitly.

Step 9.1 adds `synthesize_outbound_id: bool = True` to `Store.create_message`. Invocation-mode `send_message` (Step 7.2) passes `True` to preserve Sprint 1a synthetic-id behavior. Resident-mode `send_message` passes `False` so `discord_message_id` stays NULL until Discord confirms. `tests/test_create_message_synthesize_flag.py` (Step 20.5) locks both behaviors.

Same as callers: the `synthesize_outbound_id` parameter is the explicit caller-level switch. Step 11.2 modifies `SQLiteStore.create_message` (`agent_kit/store/sqlite.py:101`) to gate `_next_invocation_message_id` behind the new flag, with `True` as the default so Sprint 1a behavior and tests are unchanged.

last_gate.rationale: Iteration 4 with weighted score 9.0 (down from 35.0 → 17.5 → 11.0 → 9.0), 28/35 prior flags resolved, plan delta 49%, zero recurring/reopened critiques. All 6 remaining flags describe the SAME single issue: ingestion-side Supabase Storage uploads don't have explicit source-byte material in `request_body` for crash-before-upload reissue. This is a real but bounded plan-quality observation that I'm accepting as a documented tradeoff with explicit fallback semantics. Rationale for accept_tradeoff: (a) The failure window is narrow — crash strictly between persisting the `messages`+ledger transaction and finishing the Storage upload. (b) The inbound `messages` row IS durable across the crash (the iteration-3 persist-first design ensures that), so the user is never silently dropped. (c) The Discord CDN attachment URL is naturally available at ingestion time and is the obvious source material — the plan should add `request_body={"discord_attachment_url": <attachment.url>, "deterministic_path": ...}` for ingestion Storage rows and have the reconciler fetch from that URL when reissuing. Discord attachment URLs do expire (~24h with new signed-URL rules), so the reconciler must `mark_orphaned` + `system_logs` warn entry on fetch failure. This is straightforward to spec in implementation and is the implementer's call within the existing plan structure. (d) Standard robustness budget at iteration 4 with score 9.0 doesn't justify another full iteration to add 2-3 sentences to Step 13.4 and Step 16; the implementer can resolve this design point during execution. (e) Iteration Pressure Analysis: FG-001..FG-005 each have 1 member flag across 4 iterations and addressed_then_reopened = 0 — does not meet TIEBREAKER threshold (which requires ≥2 reopened OR ≥2 member flags across ≥2 iterations). The pattern is "new edge cases each iteration," not "same flag re-litigated" — typical late-iteration nitpicker drift. Settle the Storage recovery design as listed below and move to execution.
last_gate.signals_assessment: Continued strong improvement with diminishing flag count: weighted score 35.0 → 17.5 → 11.0 → 9.0 (-74% cumulative), plan delta 49% with the change concentrated on one specific subsystem each iteration, 28 prior flags resolved with zero reopened. The 6 unresolved flags this iteration all describe a single design point (ingestion Storage upload source-byte durability) and represent new visibility, not recurring churn — typical late-iteration narrowing. Iteration Pressure analysis shows FG-001..FG-005 each at 1 member flag × 4 iterations × 0 reopened, which does not meet TIEBREAKER thresholds (need ≥2 member flags or ≥2 reopened). Preflight clean. No new debt subsystems escalated. Score 9.0 is low enough at iteration 4 that the marginal cost of another iteration outweighs the value, especially since the resolution is a documented design tradeoff that the implementer can apply directly.
warnings: Implementer must add `discord_attachment_url` to `request_body` on ingestion Storage pending rows so the reconciler has a fetch source. If `Blob.exists(deterministic_path)` is False at reconciler time AND the Discord URL fetch fails (expired/404), the row must be `mark_orphaned` with a `system_logs` warn entry at category=recovery — do NOT silently retry forever.
Add a smoke test that simulates crash-before-Storage-upload to verify the orphaned + log path works end-to-end, even if it's marked info-priority.

Storage recovery limitation should be noted in the Step 22.2 deferral file alongside the existing attachment debt: "Voice/image ingestion crash-before-upload + Discord URL expiry results in orphaned ledger rows; manual user re-send is the recovery path. Future sprint may add ingestion-time bytes-to-tmpfile fallback."
Service-role JWT in idea block must still be rotated by the user before this code is deployed against production Supabase.

settled_decisions.decision: Discord whitelist via env var DISCORD_USER_WHITELIST.
Supabase store uses direct psycopg3; supabase-py only for Storage.
Supabase epic lock uses row-based epic_locks table.
Mid-turn handling implements spec same-turn end-of-turn re-prompt via mid_turn_message_check hook in run_turn, gating both final-text and explicit send_message tool calls.
tests/store_contract.py UNCHANGED; new method coverage in tests/store_contract_v1b.py.
PushTransport added as a NEW Protocol alongside existing Transport.
Image and voice scope is Discord-only this sprint.
ToolContext gains optional transport, blob, external_queue fields.
Audit wrapper keeps tool body inside store.transaction(); only post-commit network callables queued.
external_requests gets nullable request_body JSONB column.
Discord ingestion-side IO is ledgered with discord_message_id-keyed idempotency.
Blob.exists and PushTransport.fetch_recent_messages published in Protocols.
Store.update_message added to Protocol; consumed by resident send_message, send_image, voice/image ingestion.
Store.create_message accepts synthesize_outbound_id flag (default True preserves Sprint 1a behavior; False used by resident send_message).

DiscordTransport.on_message persists messages row + ingestion ledger pending rows in one transaction BEFORE any external IO; update_message fills post-processing fields after each external call confirms.

Ingestion Storage pending rows include `discord_attachment_url` in request_body alongside `deterministic_path`. On reconciler reissue: if Blob.exists(path) is False, the reconciler attempts to fetch from the Discord URL and re-upload. If the Discord URL is unreachable (expired/404), the reconciler calls mark_orphaned and writes a system_logs warn entry at category=recovery. The persisted inbound `messages` row remains durable so the user can re-send.
settled_decisions.rationale: Resolves the iteration-4 Storage recovery flag cluster as accepted tradeoff. Discord URL expiry is a known limitation; orphaned + log is the documented recovery path. Future sprint may add tmpfile bytes fallback if the orphaned rate is meaningful.

last_gate.orchestrator_guidance: Plan passed gate and preflight. Proceed to finalize. Verify unresolved flags against the plan and project code before accepting.
